name: Grant Green
abstract: Grant Green (June 6, 1935 – January 31, 1979) was an American jazz guitarist and composer. Green has been called one of the "most sampled guitarists."

about_quotes.speaker: James Chirillo
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Pass and Grant Green, guys like that, of course Wes. But I hadn't even heard of Charlie Parker when I went there
about_quotes.doc_title: James Chirillo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: So we started doing some things. I started doing some things with Grant Green and Joe Henderson... All of a sudden I’m dong these things with Grant Green and stuff.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Lamb
about_quotes.evidence: He had picked up some guys in New York, all the New York musicians, including Grant Green the guitar player... The first gig was with Mercer, with his band.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Lamb interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Wendell Harrison
about_quotes.evidence: Oh when I first moved to New York I started working with Grant Green.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendell Harrison interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 10, 2022 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Chris Parker
about_quotes.evidence: Rudy Van Gelder was the engineer and you know on all those Blue Note records, I mean he was like a giant among engineers for all those Lee Morgan and Art Blakey and Grant Green I mean all those guys, Wes Montgomery records.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chris Parker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 5, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Al Gafa
about_quotes.evidence: And it was loaded with really good players, Wes, Kenny Burrell, Grant Green, George Benson, and a saxophone player named Eddie Harris. So I go in to listen to Wes and I’d listen from ten to two in the morning.
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Gafa interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 2, 2023
